name: GPT-5.1 Pro
tagline: OpenAI's research-grade ChatGPT reasoning tier, succeeded within a month
seoTitle: GPT-5.1 Pro: OpenAI's ChatGPT Pro Reasoning Model
metaDescription: GPT-5.1 Pro was OpenAI's highest-compute ChatGPT reasoning model, launched Nov 19, 2025 to replace GPT-5 Pro. Specs, access, pricing and status here.
status: Superseded. Released November 19, 2025 in ChatGPT; replaced GPT-5 Pro and was itself replaced by GPT-5.2 Pro on December 11, 2025.
license: Proprietary (OpenAI). Available through ChatGPT subscriptions under OpenAI's terms of use; not open-weight.
contextWindow: GPT-5.1 Pro ran inside ChatGPT, where OpenAI did not publish a token context limit for the Pro tier. The GPT-5.1 API model it is based on uses a 400,000-token context window.
maxOutput: Not separately published for the ChatGPT Pro tier. The GPT-5.1 API model supports up to 128,000 output tokens.
knowledgeCutoff: Not separately published for GPT-5.1 Pro. The underlying GPT-5.1 API model lists a September 30, 2024 knowledge cutoff.
architecture: Proprietary transformer-based reasoning model. As part of OpenAI's GPT Pro line, GPT-5.1 Pro uses scaled, parallel test-time compute to think for longer than the standard Thinking model before answering. OpenAI has not published the architecture details.
overview: GPT-5.1 Pro was OpenAI's highest-compute consumer reasoning model, released on November 19, 2025 as part of the GPT-5.1 family. It rolled out inside ChatGPT and replaced GPT-5 Pro in the model picker. Like the rest of the GPT Pro line, GPT-5.1 Pro is the 'thinking-first' option built for hard problems: it spends extended, parallel test-time compute to produce the most careful and reliable answers OpenAI offered at the time, trading speed for quality.

Unlike the broader GPT-5.1 release, GPT-5.1 Pro was a ChatGPT product feature rather than a published API model. It was available to ChatGPT Pro and Business subscribers through the model picker, with the Pro plan priced at $200 per month. At launch OpenAI did not ship a separate gpt-5.1-pro API endpoint; developers wanting Pro-grade compute used the existing GPT-5 Pro API line, while GPT-5.1 itself shipped to the API as the standard model. Third-party trackers noted the GPT-5.1 Pro API had not been officially released.

GPT-5.1 Pro had an unusually short life at the top. OpenAI released the full GPT-5.2 family on December 11, 2025 — under a month later — and GPT-5.2 Pro took over as the smartest, most trustworthy ChatGPT option for difficult questions. GPT-5.1 Pro is therefore best understood as a brief, transitional step between GPT-5 Pro and GPT-5.2 Pro during OpenAI's rapid late-2025 release cadence.
strengths: Highest-quality, most reliable answers in the GPT-5.1 family for hard, multi-step problems
Extended parallel test-time compute for careful reasoning rather than fast responses
Strong fit for science, math, and complex coding work that rewards a higher-quality answer over speed
Inherits the GPT-5.1 family's adaptive reasoning, improved instruction-following, and warmer conversational tone
Multimodal input (text and image) for analyzing documents, charts, and screenshots
useCases: Tackling difficult technical or scientific questions where answer quality matters more than latency
Complex, multi-step coding and debugging tasks
Deep research and analysis on hard reasoning problems
Reviewing and reasoning over long or intricate documents
Professional knowledge work for ChatGPT Pro and Business power users

links.label: GPT-5.1: A smarter, more conversational ChatGPT (OpenAI)

faq.a: GPT-5.1 Pro was OpenAI's highest-compute reasoning model in the GPT-5.1 family, released on November 19, 2025. It was the 'thinking-first' ChatGPT option that used extended, parallel test-time compute to give the most careful and reliable answers for hard problems, replacing GPT-5 Pro in the model picker.

faq.q: Could you use GPT-5.1 Pro through the API?
faq.a: Not at launch. GPT-5.1 Pro was a ChatGPT product feature for Pro and Business subscribers, not a published API model. OpenAI shipped standard GPT-5.1 to the API, but third-party trackers noted that a dedicated GPT-5.1 Pro API endpoint had not been officially released.

faq.q: How much did GPT-5.1 Pro cost?
faq.a: Access was through the ChatGPT Pro subscription at $200 per month (it was also available to Business users). OpenAI did not publish separate per-token API pricing for GPT-5.1 Pro; the underlying GPT-5.1 API model was priced at $1.25 per million input tokens and $10 per million output tokens.
No. GPT-5.1 Pro was superseded quickly: OpenAI released the GPT-5.2 family on December 11, 2025, under a month after launch, and GPT-5.2 Pro took over as the top ChatGPT reasoning model.

pricing.note: GPT-5.1 Pro was a ChatGPT product feature, not a published API model. Access was via the ChatGPT Pro subscription at $200/month (also available to Business users); OpenAI did not publish per-token API pricing for a gpt-5.1-pro endpoint. The underlying GPT-5.1 API model was priced at $1.25/M input and $10/M output tokens.
